questions.title: Two Sum
questions.description:
Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
You can return the answer in any order.

examples.example_text:
Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9
Output: [0,1]
Explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].

hints: Use hash table for O(1) lookup

questions.title: Database Normalization
questions.description:
Explain the concept of database normalization and normalize the given table to 3NF.

Given table: Student(StudentID, Name, Course1, Course2, Course3, Instructor1, Instructor2, Instructor3)

Normalize this table to eliminate redundancy and ensure data integrity.

examples.example_text:
Input: Unnormalized table with repeating groups
Output: Multiple normalized tables in 3NF
Explanation: Break down into Student, Course, and Enrollment tables

questions.title: Process Scheduling Algorithms
questions.description:
Implement and compare different CPU scheduling algorithms: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, and Priority Scheduling.

Given a set of processes with arrival times, burst times, and priorities, calculate average waiting time and turnaround time for each algorithm.

examples.example_text:
Input: Processes = [(P1, 0, 5, 3), (P2, 1, 3, 1), (P3, 2, 8, 2)]
Format: (ProcessID, ArrivalTime, BurstTime, Priority)
Output: Average waiting time and turnaround time for each algorithm

follow_ups: Which algorithm is best for interactive systems?

questions.title: TCP vs UDP
questions.description:
Compare TCP and UDP protocols. Explain when to use each protocol and implement a simple client-server communication using both.

Discuss the trade-offs between reliability and performance.

examples.example_text:
Scenario: Real-time video streaming
Question: Which protocol would you choose and why?
Answer: UDP for low latency, accepting some packet loss

code_snippets.python:
import socket

# TCP Server
def tcp_server():
    server = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    # Implement TCP server
    pass

# UDP Server
def udp_server():
    server = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
    # Implement UDP server
    pass

questions.title: Binary Search Tree Operations
questions.description:
Implement a Binary Search Tree with insert, delete, search, and traversal operations.

Ensure the BST property is maintained after each operation.

examples.example_text:
Input: Insert [5, 3, 7, 2, 4, 6, 8]
Output: BST with proper structure
Operations: search(4) -> True, delete(3) -> restructure tree

constraints: Handle edge cases like deleting root

questions.title: SQL Query Optimization
questions.description:
Given a slow-performing SQL query, identify performance bottlenecks and optimize it.

Consider indexing strategies, query rewriting, and execution plan analysis.

examples.example_text:
Slow Query:
SELECT * FROM orders o, customers c, products p 
WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND o.product_id = p.id 
AND c.city = 'New York' AND p.price > 100

Optimize this query for better performance.

code_snippets.sql:
-- Original slow query
SELECT * FROM orders o, customers c, products p 
WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND o.product_id = p.id 
AND c.city = 'New York' AND p.price > 100;

-- Optimized query
-- Your solution here

questions.title: Memory Management
questions.description:
Implement different memory allocation algorithms: First Fit, Best Fit, and Worst Fit.

Simulate memory allocation and deallocation, handling fragmentation.

examples.example_text:
Memory blocks: [100, 500, 200, 300, 600]
Requests: [212, 417, 112, 426]
Show allocation for each algorithm and calculate fragmentation.

questions.title: Network Routing Algorithms
questions.description:
Implement Dijkstra's algorithm for shortest path routing in a network.

Given a network topology with link costs, find the shortest path between any two nodes.

examples.example_text:
Network: A-B(4), A-C(2), B-C(1), B-D(5), C-D(8), C-E(10), D-E(2)
Find shortest path from A to E
Output: A -> C -> B -> D -> E with cost 10

code_snippets.python:

    distances = {node: float('inf') for node in graph}
    distances[start] = 0

questions.title: ACID Properties
questions.description:
Explain ACID properties with practical examples. Implement a simple transaction manager that ensures ACID compliance.

Demonstrate how violations of each property can lead to data inconsistency.

questions.title: Deadlock Detection
questions.description:
Implement deadlock detection using resource allocation graphs.

Given processes and their resource requests, detect if a deadlock exists and suggest resolution strategies.

examples.example_text:
Processes: P1, P2, P3
Resources: R1, R2, R3
Allocations: P1->R1, P2->R2, P3->R3
Requests: P1->R2, P2->R3, P3->R1
Result: Deadlock detected (circular wait)
